converter.hsvNote: HSV is for reference. CSS has no hsv() function — use HSL.

palette.imageDrop: Drop image here or click to upload (max 5MB)

errors.fileNotImage: Please upload a PNG, JPG, or WebP image

descriptions.title: About Color Conversion
descriptions.introP1: This color tool converts between 7 color spaces, generates harmonies, checks contrast ratios (WCAG and APCA), simulates color blindness, and extracts palettes from images. See [Number Base converter](/numbase) for hex calculations, or [Image compressor](/image) for image processing.
descriptions.spaceHex: HEX — compact 6/8-digit RGB notation. The default for CSS.
descriptions.spaceRgb: RGB — additive primaries 0–255. Maps directly to display pixels.
descriptions.spaceHsl: HSL — hue/saturation/lightness. Easier for humans to reason about than RGB.
descriptions.spaceHsv: HSV — hue/saturation/value. Common in design tools but not a CSS function.
descriptions.spaceCmyk: CMYK — subtractive primaries used in print. Approximate when converted from RGB.
descriptions.spaceLab: LAB — perceptually uniform; equal numeric distance ≈ equal visual distance.
descriptions.spaceOklch: OKLCH — modern perceptual space; preferred by Tailwind v4 and CSS Color Module Level 4.
descriptions.contrastP1: WCAG 2.1 uses a luminance ratio (1:1 to 21:1) with thresholds for AA/AAA. It is widely supported but doesn't fully account for perception of small text or dark mode.
descriptions.contrastP2: APCA is a candidate for WCAG 3 with a polarity-aware Lc score. It correlates better with readability for thin or small text.
descriptions.visionP1: Roughly 1 in 12 men and 1 in 200 women have a form of color vision deficiency. The Vision toggle simulates protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia, and achromatopsia so you can verify your design holds up.
descriptions.tipEyedropper: Use the eyedropper to pick a color from any pixel on your screen (Chromium browsers).
descriptions.tipImage: Drop an image into the Palette tab to extract its 6 dominant colors via median-cut quantization.
descriptions.tipOklch: Tailwind v4 emits colors in OKLCH for perceptual smoothness — copy the @theme block to align with that workflow.
descriptions.aeoDefinition: Color Converter is a free online tool for converting between HEX, RGB, HSL, OKLCH and other color spaces. Includes a visual picker, image palette extraction, WCAG contrast checker, and color-blindness preview. Runs entirely in your browser.
descriptions.faq1A: The Color Tool is a comprehensive color utility that supports HEX, RGB, HSL, and OKLCH color models with a visual picker, conversions, and contrast checking.
descriptions.faq2A: OKLCH is a perceptually uniform color space that better matches human vision than HSL or RGB. It produces more consistent lightness gradients and accessible palettes.
descriptions.faq3Q: Can I extract colors from an image?
descriptions.faq3A: Yes. Drag and drop any image onto the palette extraction area to get the dominant colors. You can also simulate color vision deficiencies (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia).
